name: Vinegar and Your Pots and Pans
recipeInstructions:
1. Fill aluminum pots and pans with water and add 2 tablespoons of vinegar. Bring to a boil, then turn off heat and rinse with cool water.
2. Add 1 teaspoon of vinegar to each pot before use to prevent mineral deposits. The amount won't affect cooking flavors but will prevent buildup over time.
3. For burned-on mess in broiler pan, mix 2 cups vinegar with 1/4 cup sugar and place in still-hot pan. Let soak for an hour, then clean as usual.
4. To remove rust from iron skillet, fill with 1/4 cup vinegar and 1/4 cup water. Boil on stove, then turn off heat. Continue cooking for another half hour before pouring out the mixture and wiping surface clean.
5. For copper pots, mix equal parts flour and salt with enough vinegar to form a thick paste. Rub onto copper and let sit for five minutes before rinsing with cool water and drying.
6. Wipe copper pots down between uses with a solution of 1 part vinegar to 1 part salt.
